surface.name: Trial Account Onboarding
surface.selection_basis: PagerDuty's support home places Trial Account Onboarding in Getting Started, and that guide explicitly covers a brand-new account from work-email eligibility and tenant creation through six onboarding sections and the terminal redirect into the account. It is broader and earlier than API-key, service-integration, or incident-operation guides.

alternatives_considered.name: Services and Integrations
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: This path assumes an existing account, role, and notification model; the trial onboarding guide creates the first service and can select an inbound integration earlier.

alternatives_considered.name: REST API and API Access Keys
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: This developer route assumes an existing PagerDuty account and can require administrator permission; the general new-account guide is the documented platform entry.

alternatives_considered.name: Events API v2
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: Sending an event requires a service-level integration key created after service and integration setup, so it is downstream of the selected onboarding surface.

entry_point.developer_intent: Create a new PagerDuty trial tenant and complete the official onboarding flow into a configured account.
assumed_prior_state: Discovery is complete and the developer has selected PagerDuty.
The developer does not have a PagerDuty account and has an eligible work email address.

documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: A new PagerDuty trial tenant exists with its first service, tested notification methods, the mobile-app step completed, optional schedule and integration choices resolved, and the onboarding flow has redirected the developer into the PagerDuty account.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: After the final Connect With Your Chat Tools step is completed or skipped, the guide says the developer is directed to the PagerDuty account; the created first service is available there, and the earlier test notifications were received through the selected methods.

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: The Trial Account Onboarding guide states that after completing the final step, the developer is directed to the PagerDuty account, then lists only post-onboarding resources.

documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: This is the terminal state of PagerDuty's official brand-new-account onboarding flow. Sending a real monitoring event, creating an API key, adding more services, and configuring incident behavior are post-onboarding paths.

prerequisites.requirement: An eligible work email address; common consumer domains such as gmail.com, yahoo.com, and icloud.com are restricted from trial signup.
First name, last name, company, country, and a password for the new account.
A choice of USA or EU service region, made during tenant creation.
A unique PagerDuty subdomain at least five characters long.
A reachable phone capable of voice and SMS plus an email inbox for the guide's recommended all-three-method test; a supported mobile device is needed for the mobile-app step.

primary_path.action: Open Trial Account Onboarding and follow its signup link.
details: The guide is explicitly for getting started with a new PagerDuty account.
primary_path.input: Intent to start a PagerDuty trial
primary_path.success_signal: The Work Email field is visible.

primary_path.action: Enter an eligible Work Email and click Get Started.
details: Consumer email domains such as gmail.com, yahoo.com, and icloud.com are restricted.
primary_path.success_signal: The First Name, Last Name, Company, and Country fields appear.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: A restricted non-work email cannot create the trial and must be replaced with an eligible work address.

primary_path.action: Enter First Name, Last Name, Company, and Country, then click Next Step.
Enter a password and click Next Step.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: A password rejected by the current form must be corrected; the public onboarding guide does not enumerate password rules.

primary_path.action: Enter a personalized PagerDuty subdomain that is at least five characters long and not already in use.
details: The subdomain becomes the tenant's personalized PagerDuty web address.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: A value shorter than five characters or already taken must be replaced.

primary_path.action: Select USA or EU as the service region.
details: This determines where PagerDuty hosts the account.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The guide does not document changing this region after account creation.

primary_path.action: Review the Terms of Service and Privacy Policy, check the acknowledgment and agreement box if accepted, then click Create Account.
primary_path.input: Completed tenant form and policy acceptance
primary_path.success_signal: The onboarding flow opens at Step 1: Create a Service.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Without policy acceptance, the account cannot be created.

primary_path.action: Enter a descriptive Service Name for a service the team owns and click Continue.
details: The guide examples include Account Authentication, Checkout App Server, and Inventory Database.
primary_path.success_signal: Onboarding advances to Configure Inbound Integrations.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: No service-name validation rules or creation retry are documented on the page.

primary_path.action: At Configure Inbound Integrations, click Skip step for the neutral first path.
details: Selecting one or more integrations is optional and requires a tool-specific guide after onboarding.
primary_path.output: No inbound integration selected during onboarding
primary_path.success_signal: Onboarding advances to Configure Notification Methods.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: A developer selecting an integration must later follow its separate configuration guide before that tool can send data.

primary_path.action: Check Phone & SMS and enter a phone number; check Email and enter an email address.
details: The guide recommends setting up email, voice call, and SMS during initial account setup to test all three.
primary_path.input: Reachable phone number and email address
primary_path.success_signal: Phone, SMS, and email destinations are shown in the notification test form.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The entire notification-method step can be skipped, but doing so removes the guide's recommended test evidence.

primary_path.success_signal: PagerDuty asks the developer to confirm the details.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Invalid contact details must be corrected before sending.

primary_path.action: Confirm the details are correct and click Send Test Notification again.
primary_path.output: Dispatched test voice, SMS, and email notifications
primary_path.success_signal: PagerDuty reports that the tests were sent.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Delivery can take time outside the web flow; incorrect details require correction and another send.

primary_path.action: Receive and verify the test notifications through the configured methods.
details: The guide states that test notifications are received through the methods configured.
primary_path.success_signal: The developer receives the test email, voice call, and SMS.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The onboarding page does not provide a delivery-time claim or detailed retry instructions; contact-method troubleshooting is linked as post-onboarding guidance.

primary_path.action: Download the PagerDuty mobile app from the listed Apple App Store or Google Play link.
details: The device platform determines which store is used.
primary_path.input: Supported mobile device and store account
primary_path.success_signal: The app is present on the device.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Store access, device compatibility, download, or installation failures block this onboarding step; no skip control is documented in the page text.

primary_path.action: Click Next after the mobile-app step.
Click Skip step at Configure an On-Call Schedule for the single-developer neutral route.
details: The full path can instead invite users and create the first schedule and Default escalation policy.
primary_path.output: No additional user invitation or schedule during onboarding
primary_path.success_signal: Onboarding advances to Connect With Your Chat Tools.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Choosing to create a schedule requires another person's first name, last name, and email and dispatches an external invitation.

primary_path.action: At Connect With Your Chat Tools, choose to add extensions later and complete the final step without adding one.
details: The guide explicitly allows adding chat extensions later.
primary_path.input: Optional Slack or Microsoft Teams integration decision
primary_path.output: Completed onboarding without a chat extension
primary_path.success_signal: PagerDuty directs the developer to the account.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Selecting Add Extensions adds an extension search and external authentication workflow.

primary_path.action: Confirm the final redirect opens the new PagerDuty account and the first service is available.
details: The guide now labels all linked setup material as Post-Onboarding.
primary_path.success_signal: The account UI loads under the selected subdomain and region with the created service, after notification tests have succeeded.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The public guide does not document a redirect-retry action; reauthenticate to the personalized subdomain if the session is lost.

branches.condition: The email uses a restricted consumer domain.
branches.path: Replace it with an eligible work email and retry Get Started.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds an identity correction before any account details can be entered.

branches.condition: The requested subdomain is too short or already in use.
branches.path: Enter another unique subdomain of at least five characters and retry validation.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds tenant-naming retries before region and policy acceptance.

branches.condition: The developer wants an inbound tool integration during onboarding.
branches.path: Select one or more integrations from Most Popular or All, click Continue, then after onboarding follow each selected integration's linked guide to complete its external configuration.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds a tool choice during onboarding and a separate post-onboarding configuration journey before real events can arrive.

branches.path: Click Skip step and configure contact methods and notification rules later.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Removes the test-notification dispatch and delivery evidence from onboarding.

branches.condition: The mobile device is iOS or iPadOS.
branches.path: Use the listed Apple App Store link to download the PagerDuty mobile app, then click Next.
Use the listed Google Play link to download the PagerDuty mobile app, then click Next.

branches.condition: The developer creates the first on-call schedule now.
branches.path: For each user, enter First Name, Last Name, and Email, use the add control to invite and add that user, click Create Schedule, wait for the confirmation that the schedule and escalation policy were created and invitations sent, then click Continue.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds personal-data entry, one or more external invitations, a schedule, a Default escalation policy, a confirmation dialog, and possible coordination wait.

branches.condition: The developer adds Slack, Microsoft Teams, or another chat extension now.
branches.path: Click Add Extensions, on the Extensions page click Add under a popular extension or New Extension to search, then complete the selected extension's external authentication workflow.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds a tool choice, permission grant, and an external authentication journey before the terminal account redirect.

branches.condition: The developer skips the entire onboarding flow.
branches.path: Use the Post-Onboarding resources linked after each section to create a service, configure integrations, contact methods, schedules, escalation policy, mobile access, and extensions separately.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Replaces the guided sequence with several independent setup guides and loses one terminal onboarding redirect.

friction_gates.description: PagerDuty trial signup rejects common consumer email domains and requires a work identity plus company and country data.
The developer must secure a unique personalized subdomain at least five characters long.
USA or EU data-hosting region must be selected before account creation.
Terms of Service and Privacy Policy must be reviewed and affirmatively accepted to create the account.
Inbound integration selection is optional but tool-specific; selecting one moves real event delivery into a separate guide.
The guide recommends configuring and testing email, voice, and SMS, requiring reachable external channels and successful delivery.
The onboarding sequence includes downloading the mobile app from one of two external stores before clicking Next.
Creating a schedule now dispatches teammate invitations and creates a Default escalation policy; skipping defers both.
Adding a chat extension requires an external authentication workflow; the neutral path defers it.

sources.title: Trial Account Onboarding
sections_used: Step 6: Connect With Your Chat Tools

sources.title: Services and Integrations
sections_used: Add Integrations to an Existing Service
Create a Generic Events API Integration
evidence_supported: Service and integration path is downstream

sources.title: API Access Keys
sections_used: Generate a General Access REST API Key
Generate a User Token REST API Key
evidence_supported: REST and Events API alternative surfaces

uncertainties.question: What password rules does the current trial signup form enforce?
uncertainties.impact: A rejected password can add one or more correction attempts before subdomain selection.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The official onboarding guide says to enter a password but does not publish the validation rules on that page.

uncertainties.question: What is the missing label of the control used to add each invited user during schedule setup?
uncertainties.impact: A developer taking the optional schedule branch must identify the correct add action before Create Schedule.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The official rendered guide's sentence contains an unlabeled control placeholder between entering Email and inviting the user, though the required inputs and resulting invitation are explicit.

uncertainties.question: Is there a current UI skip control for the mobile-app step?
uncertainties.impact: If the developer cannot install the app, the onboarding path may be blocked or may have an undocumented skip control.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The official page explicitly documents Download and Next but, unlike other optional sections, does not say that this step can be skipped.

excluded_after_success: Completing a selected inbound integration's separate guide
Sending the first real Events API event
Schedule and escalation-policy customization after a skipped step
